rulings.Case Title: BOSCO CREDIT LLC VS. GEET PRASAD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Monetary Judgment On The Grounds Said Judgment Is Void, Is Violative Of Due Process Principals, And Is The Result Of Extrinsic Fraud And Mistake
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 30, 2018, Line 1, DEFENDANTs GEET PRASAD and MAHENDRA PRASAD's Motion To Set Aside Monetary Judgment On The Grounds Said Judgment Is Void, Is Violative Of Due Process Principals, And Is The Result Of Extrinsic Fraud And Mistake Defendants Geet and Mahendra Prasad's motion to vacate the August 15, 2017 judgment is granted based on CCP 473(d) and extrinsic mistake. The judgment is set aside and this case is restored to the civil active list. The record discloses that the Prasads had no notice that plaintiff Bosco Credit, LLC was attempting to enter judgment against them. In addition, the judgment improperly awards plaintiff nearly $70,000 when the stipulation only allowed for a judgment of $20,000. The stipulation does not permit plaintiff to seek the full amount of the alleged debt in the event of a default. Even if the stipulation provided for such relief, the recovery of nearly $70,000 would be an illegal penalty per Greentree Financial Group, Inc. v. Execute Sports, Inc. (2008) 163 Cal.App.4th 495. While this motion could have been brought sooner, counsel for the Prasads provides an acceptable explanation for the delay. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Prasads is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: TYLER JOHNSTON VS. SYNERGY PROJECT MANAGEMENT, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 30, 2018, Line 2, CROSS DEFENDANT CENTRAL STRIPING SERVICE, INC. DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT Cross-Defendant Central Striping Service, Inc.'s demurrer to all five causes of action in the cross-complaint filed by Synergy Project Management is continued to December 4, 2018 and the court, per CCP 128.7(c)(2) issues an order for Synergy's counsel to show cause in writing no later than November 27, 2018 why they have not violated CCP 128.7(b) by filing and continuing to prosecute Synergy's cross-complaint. The conduct of Synergy's counsel that appears to violate CCP 128.7(b) is the filing and continued prosecution of a cross-complaint that is barred by well-established law. CSS has no obligation to indemnify Synergy against claims brought by Mr. Johnston when CSS has provided workers compensation. "[A]n employer that provides compensation coverage has no further liability for workplace injuries to an employee. Therefore, if a nonnegligent third party pays damages for an employee's injuries that are attributable in whole or in part to the negligence of the employer, the Act's limitations on employer liability preclude the third party from obtaining equitable indemnity from the employer." (Privette v. Superior Court (1993) 5 Cal.4th 689, 698 (citations omitted).) Contractual claims by third parties are likewise barred if they are collateral to or derivative of an injury covered by workers compensation. (Vacanti v. State Comp. Ins. Fund (2001) 24 Cal.4th 800, 814.) It appears that CSS has provided workers compensation coverage for Mr. Johnston, so it does not also need to indemnify Synergy for any of CSS's alleged negligence that contributed to the accident. Unless Synergy shows cause why it has not violated CCP 128.7(b) or dismisses the cross-complaint, on December 4, 2018 the court intends to dismiss the cross-complaint per CCP 128.7(d). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for CSS is required to prepare a proposed order entitled "Order to Show Cause and Continuing Hearing On Demurrer" which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: JOHN DOE VS. RAMLA CO., LTD., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Court Order To Allow Pretrial Discovery Of Defendant'S Financial Condition In Support Of A Punitive Damage Claim; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 30, 2018, Line 7, PLAINTIFF JOHN DOE's Motion For A Court Order To Allow Pretrial Discovery Of Defendant'S Financial Condition In Support Of A Punitive Damage Claim Plaintiff John Doe's motion for a court order to allow pretrial discovery of defendant's financial condition is granted in part. The court adopts in full the recommendations and reasons for those recommendations in the report of Judge Pro Tem Roger Mead. Although plaintiff did not explicitly request the relief recommended by Mr. Mead, that relief is explicitly provided for in the statute and can fairly be described as "lesser included" relief of the relief explicitly sought by plaintiff. The court discerns no legal prejudice to defendant Ramla Co., Ltd. by granting the relief recommended by Mr. Mead. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and attaches the report of Judge Pro Tem Roger Mead and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk ____________________________________________________________________________

rulings.Case Title: BRYANT FU ET AL VS. TINA YAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Defendant Stella Chen'S Demurrer To Liu'S Cross Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 30, 2018, Line 9, Cross Defendant Stella Chen'S Demurrer To Liu'S Cross Complaint Hearing required. While all grounds for the demurrer to Kaman Liu's cross-complaint appear to lack merit except for another action pending, the court is concerned whether it has the authority to rule on this demurrer due to a possible bankruptcy stay. If plaintiff Tony Fu's bankruptcy case is still pending and if Mr. Fu fraudulently transferred assets, wouldn't those assets be part of his bankruptcy estate? Counsel should be prepared to address that question at the hearing. Counsel should also address whether the fraudulent transfer claims need to be prosecuted in CGC-16-553702 or whether Mr. Liu should dismiss his cross-complaint in that action as to cross-defendant Stella Chen and prosecute the claims in this action. Ms. Chen's remaining arguments are not persuasive at this point of the litigation. Ms. Chen fails to show that the cross-complaint and judicially noticeable facts necessarily show that the 4-year statute of limitations bars the claims against her. Florence Fung and Yuet Ho are alleged to be the assignors in this case and the face of the cross-complaint does not reveal their inquiry knowledge regarding the claims. The 4-year statute of limitations of CC 3439.09(a) is subject to the discovery rule. Ms. Chen improperly raises the statute of repose of CC 3439.09(c) for the first time in the reply papers. In addition, Ms. Chen misreads the statute. The "obligation" that the statute refers to is the transfer or obligation incurred to avoid the debt-not the underlying obligation to the creditor. Ms. Chen's argument that the fraudulent transfer claims need to be pleaded with more particularity fails. The "fraud" is in the nature of concealment and Ms. Chen should have knowledge of her alleged misdoings. Less particularity is required where the cross-defendant must necessarily possess full knowledge concerning the facts of the controversy. (Simons v. County of Kern (1965) 234 Cal.App.2d 362, 367; Committee on Children's Television, Inc. v. General Foods Corp. (1983) 35 Cal.3d 197, 217 (superseded by statute on other grounds).)

rulings.Case Title: BRYANT FU ET AL VS. TINA YAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Defendant Stella Chen'S Demurrer To Chiu'S Cross Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 30, 2018, Line 9, Cross Defendant Stella Chen'S Demurrer To Chiu'S Cross Complaint Hearing required. While all grounds for the demurrer to Thai Chiu's cross-complaint appear to lack merit, the court is concerned whether it has the authority to rule on this demurrer due to a possible bankruptcy stay. If plaintiff Tony Fu's bankruptcy case is still pending and if Mr. Fu fraudulently transferred assets, wouldn't those assets be part of his bankruptcy estate? Counsel should be prepared to address that question at the hearing. Counsel should also address whether the fraudulent transfer claims should be prosecuted in CGC-16-553702 or whether Mr. Liu should dismiss his cross-complaint in that action as to cross-defendant Stella Chen and prosecute the claims in this action. Ms. Chen's remaining arguments are not persuasive at this point of the litigation. Another action pending doesn't apply to Ms. Chiu since she is not a party to case 553702. Ms. Chen fails to show that the cross-complaint and judicially noticeable facts necessarily show that the 4-year statute of limitations bars the claims against her. Florence Fung and Yuet Ho are alleged to be the assignors in this case and the face of the cross-complaint does not reveal their inquiry knowledge regarding the claims. The 4-year statute of limitations of CC 3439.09(a) is subject to the discovery rule. Ms. Chen improperly raises the statute of repose of CC 3439.09(c) for the first time in the reply papers. In addition, Ms. Chen misreads the statute. The "obligation" that the statute refers to is the transfer or obligation incurred to avoid the debt-not the underlying obligation to the creditor. Ms. Chen's argument that the fraudulent transfer claims need to be pleaded with more particularity fails. The "fraud" is in the nature of concealment and Ms. Chen should have knowledge of her alleged misdoings. Less particularity is required where the cross-defendant must necessarily possess full knowledge concerning the facts of the controversy. (Simons v. County of Kern (1965) 234 Cal.App.2d 362, 367; Committee on Children's Television, Inc. v. General Foods Corp. (1983) 35 Cal.3d 197, 217 (superseded by statute on other grounds).) =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: SHANE STEVENS VS. LLOYD BROWN, DBA LLOYD BROWN SECURITY, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 30, 2018, Line 3, DEFENDANT BIG BILLY, INC. DBA SLIM'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Big Billy Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. There are triable disputes whether: 1) Big Billy acted negligently in hiring defendant Lloyd Brown to provide security services at the 2016 HSBF; 2) Lloyd Brown acted negligently in hiring and retaining defendants Jose Beltran and William Douglass; and 3) Mr. Brown's asserted negligence in hiring and retaining Mr. Beltran and Mr. Douglass was a substantial factor in causing plaintiff Shane Stevens' injuries. Liberally construing the evidence in favor of Mr. Stevens and drawing all reasonable inferences in favor of Mr. Stevens, a reasonable juror could conclude that: 1) Big Billy acted negligently by knowingly hiring an unlicensed PPO in violation of the requirement of Bus. & Prof. Code 7582.3 or by failing to conduct a sufficient inquiry whether Mr. Brown had the required license; 2) Mr. Brown acted negligently by failing to conduct a sufficient inquiry into the license status, training and qualifications of Mr. Beltran and Mr. Douglass and by failing to provide them with sufficient training regarding the use of force; and 3) Mr. Brown's failure to ensure that Mr. Beltran and Mr. Douglass had sufficient training and qualifications regarding use of force was a substantial factor in Mr. Beltran and Mr. Douglass using force on Mr. Stevens which caused Mr. Stevens' injuries. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Stevens is required to prepare a proposed order which complies with CCP 437c(g) and repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: BETINA SCHONBERGER ET AL VS. SCHERMCO, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 30, 2018, Line 4, CROSS-DEFENDANTS DAVID STRAIGHT AND STRAIGHT BUILDERS, INC'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT Cross-defendants Straight Builders, Inc. and David Straight's demurrer to all three causes of action in the first amended cross-complaint is overruled as to all three causes of action. Cross-complainants adequately alleged contract and equitable indemnity claims against Straight Builders and also adequately allege alter ego liability against Mr. Straight. Liberally construed, the first amended cross-complaint sufficiently alleges negligent construction by Straight Builders that caused harm to plaintiffs that could support liability by Straight Builders to the plaintiffs supporting the equitable indemnity cross-claim. Also, liberally construed, the first amended cross-complaint sufficiently alleges diversion of funds and purposeful dissolution of Straight Builders that could support alter ego liability against Mr. Straight. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for cross-complainants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK
